title: Medicago biancae
taxonomy: Medicago biancae is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It is native to the Mediterranean region, specifically in Italy. The classification of Medicago biancae is as follows: kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Fabales, family Fabaceae, genus Medicago, and species M. biancae. Within the genus Medicago, M. biancae is closely related to other species such as M. arborescens and M. citrina. Further research is needed to confirm the exact relationships between these species.

intro: Medicago biancae, also known as Bianca's clover, is a perennial legume belonging to the Fabaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Medicago biancae include isoflavones, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Bianca's clover include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
